item.title: SpazioTalk #189: the route of the Tour de France 2026
item.description: SpazioTalk returns with episode 189, an unusual midweek edition, to discuss the Tour de France 2026. After last episode’s analysis of the favourites, today we focus on the 21 stages that will shape the Grande Boucle. There are two time trials, one team and one individual, plus plenty of climbing through the Pyrenees, Massif Central, Vosges and Alps. The show promises plenty of action, and we highlight some key points that could decide the race, including the double Alpe d’Huez in the final stages.

item.title: Lidl-Trek to race a commemorative bike at the Tour de France 2026
item.description: The Tour de France 2026 will be special for Lidl-Trek. The team will celebrate 50 years of the bike maker’s history on the roads of the Grande Boucle and later at the Tour de France Femmes. For the occasion, Trek has prepared a unique frame that traces the company’s history since 1976, with the various brands, colors and symbols used over the years. The many colors will make the riders easy to spot in the bunch. The model will be called ICON 50. A responsibility, but also motivation for Mads Pedersen, ...

item.title: Junior and U23 track riders set for European Championships in Cottbus
item.description: The European Track Championships for junior and U23 riders will take place in Germany next week. On the partly covered track in Cottbus, near the Polish border, the Dutch team will mainly compete in the endurance events.

item.title: Provisional cyclocross calendar for 2026
item.description: The provisional cyclocross calendar for the 2026-2027 season has been announced. The first race is scheduled for the weekend of 26 September in Mechelen, while the first youth race is listed for 3 October in Norg.

item.title: Martinez will not target the general classification at the Tour
item.description: Last year he came close to winning the mountains classification, but in the end he had to accept that Tadej Pogačar and Jonas Vingegaard are simply out of reach in the current points system. Lenny Martinez returns to the Tour de France aiming to win the polka dot jersey, as he does not believe he can contend for the overall classification.

item.title: Seixas says he is in great shape ahead of Tour de France debut
item.description: He is likely the most closely watched debutant in this year's Tour de France. The 19-year-old Paul Seixas is expected to end France's more than 40-year wait for a home winner of the overall classification in the world's biggest race. After a strong spring, the Decathlon CMA CGM leader was among the three biggest favourites, but a crash and injury at Tour Auvergne–Rhône-Alpes lowered expectations. Seixas says there is no reason to worry, as he feels great.

item.title: Eighty national titles awarded at the 2026 USA Cycling Masters Championships
item.description: More than 800 athletes from across the country gathered in Milwaukee from June 27 to July 1 to compete for national titles in the criterium, road race, and time trial.

item.title: ATT Investments complete a clean sweep at Solidarnosc as Marceli Boguslawski wins again
item.description: The third stage of the Course Cycliste de Solidarnosc et des Champions Olympiques (2.2) was flat again, but the wind made it more selective and split the peloton. The front group kept changing, but yellow jersey Marceli Boguslawski was always missing until his teammates brought things back together for a bunch sprint. After another perfectly executed leadout, Marceli Boguslawski delivered the team’s third straight win.

Why are bikes getting faster and faster?
item.description: In just five seasons, average speeds in cycling races have soared. Much of that is down to equipment, optimized in every area. Aerodynamics, stiffness, lightness, comfort: bikes have never been so efficient. Nor have the athletes riding them. This rider-machine combination is producing astonishing speeds.

item.description: Eurosport will present the Tour de France 2026 with full live coverage on HBO Max and discovery+ and on Warner Bros. Discovery channels. Every stage of the Tour de France (4-26 July) and the Tour de France Femmes (1-9 August) will be shown. New for the 2026 edition: the exclusive Eurosport studio at the top of Alpe d'Huez and HBO Max's multi-view feature. Every day after the stage, Luca Gregorio, Riccardo Magrini, Moreno Moser and Wladimir Belli will appear on Ciclismo 360 on YouTube. Milan, 1 July – The Tour de France, the world's most famous cycling race, returns to Eurosport from Saturday 4 to Sunday 26 July with full live coverage on HBO Max and discovery+, followed by the Tour de France Femmes avec ...

item.title: Óscar Pereiro tests his memory of 2006, 20 years after his Tour win
item.description: Twenty years after the Tour that changed his life, Óscar Pereiro looks back at 2006 to see how much he remembers from that unforgettable year. From Barcelona’s Champions League title and Italy’s World Cup win to Fabio Cannavaro’s Ballon d’Or, Spain’s basketball world title, and the seasons of Nadal, Federer, Alonso and the Miami Heat, the Galician takes on MARCA’s quiz with memories, doubts and a few amusing mistakes. He even gets one cycling answer wrong, thinking Valverde won that year when it was Vinokourov.

item.title: Óscar Pereiro takes on a Tour de France mountain pass quiz 17 years after retirement
item.description: The 2026 Tour de France will climb some of the most iconic passes in the history of the Grande Boucle. Can 2006 winner Óscar Pereiro still recognize them?

item.title: Michael Boogerd explains why this Tour will still be exciting despite Tadej Pogacar
item.description: Michael Boogerd will work the full Tour de France this year as a TV commentator for NOS. He will form the regular live commentary duo on Dutch television with Andries Lamain and will also appear five times on De Avondetappe. In an interview, Boogerd explains why he is still looking forward to an exciting Tour despite Tadej Pogacar’s presence.

item.title: Michael Boogerd explains why this Tour will still be exciting despite Tadej Pogacar

item.title: Eusebio Unzué: "Movistar will continue for at least three more years"
item.description: Eusebio Unzué begins his 44th Tour with Movistar in Barcelona, says a podium finish is "unlikely," and defends a rebuilding process centered on youth, Cian, and a tight financial reality.

item.description: The Galician sits down with MARCA to reflect on the 2006 Tour, discussing Landis, the breakaway that changed his life, and the cycling that followed.

item.description: A numerical comparison between the World Cup and the Tour de France reveals some surprises in audience reach, while the sponsorship comparison between football and cycling is clear-cut.

item.description: Eusebio Unzué, 71, will be at the Grande Boucle with Movistar, continuing a run that dates back to 1983. Speaking about the team’s future, he conveyed calm and confidence.

item.title: Netcompany Ineos target Tour de France stages with strong opening team time trial lineup
item.description: Netcompany Ineos are heading to the Tour de France focused mainly on stage wins, with the opening team time trial offering their first major opportunity. The British team brings a powerful time trial squad to Barcelona, including Filippo Ganna, Joshua Tarling, Tobias Foss, Thymen Arensman, Dorian Godon, Kévin Vauquelin, Egan Bernal and Michał Kwiatkowski.

item.title: Marceli Boguslawski wins both opening half-stages of Course Cycliste de Solidarnosc et des Champions Olympiques
item.description: Course Cycliste de Solidarnosc et des Champions Olympiques (2.2) opened with two half-stages. Both ended in bunch sprints, and both were won by ATT Investments sprinter Marceli Boguslawski after strong team performances. With three days still to race, the Polish rider leads the yellow and white jersey classifications, while the Czech team tops the team standings. The two wins raise ATT Investments' season total in UCI races to nine and add 16 more UCI points.

item.title: Lidl-Trek balance Juan Ayuso's podium bid with Mads Pedersen's green jersey goal at Tour de France
item.description: Lidl-Trek head into this year's Tour de France with major ambitions on several fronts. The team wants to guide Juan Ayuso to the podium in Paris, while also chasing stage wins and the points classification with Mads Pedersen. The question is whether both goals can work within the same team.

item.title: Visma | Lease a Bike honors Vingegaard's Triple Crown with special commemorative pin

item.description: At UAE Emirates-XRG, everyone is ready to go all in with Tadej Pogacar in the Tour de France. The Slovenian has been outstanding all year and is expected to be even better in July, backed by seven top-level teammates including Tim Wellens.

item.title: Tour 2026: Belgium has never had so much sprinting depth
item.description: Fans hoping to see Belgian success in the Tour de France should mark the flat stages. Belgium has an exceptional group of fast riders, with five sprint opportunities on paper in this Tour de France.

item.title: Analysis: Paul Seixas is good for the Tour de France – but is the Tour de France good for him?
item.description: An analysis of whether Paul Seixas benefits the Tour de France, and whether racing the Tour de France is the right move for him.

item.title: Leaving Visma has not yet brought Valter success, but it has given him insight into Tour de France leaders Martinez and Tiberi
item.description: Attila Valter hoped his move from Visma | Lease a Bike to Bahrain Victorious would let him race for himself again, but the first half of the season has not gone to plan. The 28-year-old Hungarian will miss the Tour de France, though he has gained insight into team leaders Martinez and Tiberi.

item.title: Unconventional but effective: NSN's Plan B for Biniam Girmay, including his sprint train, for the Tour de France
item.description: Biniam Girmay surprisingly won three stages and the green jersey in the Tour de France two years ago. After an 18-month dry spell, the Eritrean has shown at NSN that he is back on track. What can be expected from him and his team in this Tour de France?

item.title: Tim van Dijke overcomes extra setback on road to Tour de France debut
item.description: Saturday 4 July will mark a new milestone in Tim van Dijke’s career. After the Giro d'Italia and Vuelta a Espana, he will make his Tour de France debut, following brother Mick. He is set to ride in support of Remco Evenepoel after earning the team’s full confidence in just a few months.

item.title: Milan Fretin gets full lead-out support for Tour de France
item.description: Jasper Philipsen (Alpecin-Premier Tech) and Tim Merlier (Soudal Quick-Step) are among the strongest sprinters in the Tour de France, but Belgian fast man Milan Fretin (Cofidis) is also targeting success. After knocking on the door for several years, Fretin now has his team’s full backing and will be supported by a complete lead-out train.

item.title: Bert Van Lerberghe on Tour de France sprint chances and the attacker Soudal Quick-Step wants to control
item.description: The Tour de France 2026 starts on Saturday in Barcelona, but the sprinters may have to wait until stage five for their first real opportunity. With a demanding opening weekend in Catalonia and the transfer into France via the Pyrenees, riders like Tim Merlier will first need to get through the early kilometers. Lead-out man Bert Van Lerberghe discussed the limited sprint chances and the attacking rider Soudal Quick-Step does not want to let go.

item.title: Lars Craps goes from long rehabilitation to Tour de France debut
item.description: Lars Craps earned a Tour de France selection after a very consistent Tour Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, riding in the shadow of Wout van Aert, Maxim Van Gils and Cian Uijtdebroeks. In Saint-Amand-Montrond he finished eighth from the breakaway, and in Crest-Voland he placed 13th after a long day out front.

item.title: Asgreen and Vandborg agree on what will decide the Tour team time trial
item.description: Constant speed, perfect turns and a united team. Brian Vandborg and Kasper Asgreen explain what it takes to win the opening stage team time trial of the Tour.

item.title: White jersey game: who can challenge Paul Seixas? Evenepoel is no longer eligible
item.description: The white jersey competition remains open, with Paul Seixas among the leading names. As a reminder, the rider wearing the white jersey earns 5 points for each day he finishes in it, while Remco Evenepoel is no longer eligible for the classification.

item.title: Tour 2026 preview: favorites for the mountains classification
item.description: Predicting the winner of the mountains classification is difficult, but that only makes it more interesting. General classification contenders can target the iconic polka-dot jersey, while attacking riders will also hope to take it. WielerFlits looks ahead at the main favorites.

item.title: ‘Pogacar seems better supported than Vingegaard’: does UAE have the edge over Visma at the Tour de France?
item.description: An analysis of whether UAE arrives at the Tour de France with a stronger supporting team around Tadej Pogacar than Visma does around Jonas Vingegaard.

item.title: Maratona dles Dolomites 2026 starts on 5 July
item.description: The Maratona dles Dolomites 2026 will start on 5 July and features prominent participants including former biathlete Dorothea Wierer, Vincenzo Nibali, Paolo Bettini and Alessandro Petacchi.

item.title: Teammate says Pogacar’s level is ‘downright depressing’
item.description: A teammate described Pogacar’s current level as ‘downright depressing,’ underlining just how dominant he appears ahead of the Tour de France.

item.description: On Saturday, 25 October 2003, the Roda JC stadium in Kerkrade hosted the finals of the Rabo Dikke Banden Races instead of football. It was one of the first times Mathieu van der Poel was seen in action in a cycling event, where he received his first prize from Michael Boogerd.

Van der Poel says he loves and hates the Tour before the Grand Départ in Barcelona
item.description: Mathieu van der Poel is one of the best Classics riders of all time, but his relationship with Grand Tours is more complicated. He now says he appreciates the Tour more as he gets older and is considering ending his racing career after 2028.

item.title: Coppa Italia delle Regioni 2026: Tommaso Dati takes overall lead after Italian Championships
item.description: Tommaso Dati is the new leader of the Coppa Italia delle Regioni 2026. After finishing second in the road race at the Italian Championships, the Team UKYO rider moved to the top of the individual standings with 180 points, ahead of Giulio Pellizzari on 164. Davide Donati is third on 110, followed by Diego Ulissi on 109 and Federico Iacomoni on 104.

item.title: Gianni Vermeersch finished Belgian Championships with a broken rib
item.description: Gianni Vermeersch finished seventh at the Belgian national championships despite crashing a few laps earlier. A hospital visit in Torhout the day after the race in Brasschaat revealed he had suffered a broken rib.

item.description: A new women’s team, Team Cranks, is set to enter Danish cycling with the aim of creating Denmark’s strongest development environment.

item.title: Magnus Cort Nielsen and Amalie Dideriksen headline Danish championship weekend
item.description: Denmark crowned national champions across road, time trial, mountain bike and trial disciplines over the weekend. On the road, Magnus Cort Nielsen won the elite men’s road race and Amalie Dideriksen took the elite women’s title. In the time trials, Kristian Egholm won the elite men’s crown and Sif Nikoline Bendix Madsen claimed the elite women’s title. In mountain bike, Sofie Heby Pedersen and Rose-Mai Damsgaard each won two national titles in XCC and XCO, while Gustav Heby Pedersen won the elite men’s XCO title and Sebastian Fini Juul took XCC gold.

item.title: Tour de France coverage index

Why Alberto Monti faded at the national championships, and why ATT Investments were not celebrating on the podium
item.description: Alberto Monti won the men's elite time trial, the only senior men's national title claimed by a Czech team. In the road race, he was the only rider to go with the early favorites from foreign teams, but after halfway he began to drop back and finished last among those who completed the race. Riders from ATT Investments reached the finish at the very front, yet had little reason to celebrate. Three podium places from the two elite races were no substitute for a single national title. Their consolation is ATT Investments' position in the UCI Europe Tour ranking, where they sit second.

item.title: Milan, Grégoire and Herregodts take major national titles as Kelderman also celebrates
item.description: National championships were also held across the rest of Europe over the weekend. UAE Emirates-XRG enjoyed a particularly successful weekend with titles in Italy, Switzerland and Portugal, while Lidl-Trek triumphed in the USA, Italy and the Czech Republic.

item.title: Tour de France debutants Paul Seixas and Olav Kooij to share Decathlon CMA CGM support as Gall misses out
item.description: Decathlon CMA CGM will not rely only on rising star Paul Seixas at this year's Tour de France. Sprinter Olav Kooij will also make his debut in the race after joining the French team with the Tour in mind. The question is whether the rest of the Decathlon lineup can support both ambitious riders.

item.title: Michael Kukrle was the top performer from the domestic conti scene at the national championships
item.description: At the national championships, Mathias Vacek again showed he is currently the strongest Czech rider, while the other elite podium finishers also underlined their quality. Looking beyond that top level, Michael Kukrle was the best rider from the Czech continental scene. He finished fifth in both the time trial and the road race. Those results reflect his strong season so far, which has made him the highest-ranked Czech rider at conti level in the UCI rankings.

item.title: Paula Blasi reportedly set to join Movistar from 2027

item.title: Jakub Otruba heads to his first Tour de France. He will help Gaviria in the sprints and get a chance in breakaways
item.description: Czech rider Jakub Otruba will line up for the Tour de France for the first time in his career. The Caja Rural-Seguros RGA rider made his Grand Tour debut at last year’s Vuelta a España, where he featured several times in long breakaways. He is expected to target similar moves in France.

item.title: Championships full of surprises and familiar names on top. Lotte Kopecky falls in Belgium, Elisa Longo Borghini wins in Italy
item.description: The women’s national championships produced several big stories over the weekend. Lotte Kopecky was beaten by Shari Bossuyt in Belgium, Lieke Nooijen won the Dutch title, and Elisa Longo Borghini confirmed her favorite status in Italy. Julia Kopecký will now wear the Czech champion’s jersey, while Viktória Chladoňová retained the Slovak title.

item.title: Pogačar already knows who will help him win a fifth Tour de France title
item.description: Tadej Pogačar is the clear favorite for this year’s Tour de France, but he will not win a fifth title without a strong team. UAE Emirates-XRG has named seven domestiques for this year, including Isaac del Toro and Adam Yates. Pavel Sivakov did not make the lineup.

item.title: Magnus Cort announces retirement, then wins first Danish title ahead of Tour de France
item.description: Magnus Cort has had a remarkable week. After being selected by Uno-X Mobility for the Tour de France, getting engaged, and announcing he will retire after this season, the 33-year-old then won the Danish national title for the first time in his career.

The Spanish Professional team will make its debut in the race with eight riders suited to different terrain and aiming for a stage win. Fernando Gaviria is the only rider in the team with previous Tour de France experience, having won two stages in 2018.

item.title: Top favorite Tim Merlier and Jasper Philipsen shut out at the Belgian National Championships: 'I knew they would go away, but...'
item.description: Top favorites Tim Merlier and Jasper Philipsen were the big names for the flat Belgian National Championships in Brasschaat, but were quickly left behind by two large groups that got away and built a lead of up to seven minutes. item.title: Kelly Druyts crowned Belgian champion after last year’s twin pregnancy

The Antwerp rider finished fifth in the sprint against the professional women and was the best elite rider without a contract. A remarkable performance for the mother of five, who was pregnant with twins a year ago.

item.title: KNWU and JOIN join forces for recreational cyclists
item.description: The KNWU and JOIN are working closely together to better support recreational cyclists in the Netherlands with training, development, and sporting goals. Through this long-term partnership, the cycling federation is combining its knowledge, network, and expertise with one of the leading training platforms for cyclists.

item.title: Cyclists face more crashes ahead of the holidays, BESIP, Dám respekt and Wolt remind road users to be careful
item.description: With warmer weather and the holidays approaching, more cyclists and scooter riders are out on the streets and roads, and crashes involving them are also increasing. Representatives of BESIP, the Czech Ministry of Transport, the Dám respekt initiative and technology company Wolt highlighted the need to follow the rules, keep a safe distance and show greater consideration among all road users at Wednesday’s awareness event, Den respektu, on Prague’s Ludvík Svoboda embankment.

item.title: Mathias Vacek says it was very hard today as he wins a third national title
item.description: The Czech and Slovak Championships in Jeseník ended with the expected victories for Mathias Vacek and Lukáš Kubiš. All the main contenders agreed that Vacek was a deserved winner of an exceptionally demanding championship. “I have to say it was really hard today. Right from the start eight of us got away, and we took turns well in the group, so the pace was very high, which was quite demanding,” Vacek said after the finish.

item.title: Mathias Vacek wins his third Czech title in Jeseník as Lukáš Kubiš also celebrates
item.description: Mathias Vacek confirmed his status as the main favorite and comfortably defended his title in the elite men’s road race at the Czech Championships in Jeseník. The Lidl-Trek rider sealed victory with an attack on the final climb before the finish and claimed his third national title. Tomáš Přidal (United Shipping) took second, while Jakub Otruba (Caja Rural-Seguros RGA) finished third. Lukáš Kubiš (Unibet Rose Rockets) became Slovak champion for the third year in a row.

item.title: Live coverage of the men’s race at the Czech and Slovak Championships in Jeseník
item.description: Follow the action from the hot Czech and Slovak Championships in Jeseník with us. Organizers moved the start of the 208-kilometer race 30 minutes earlier, but extreme conditions and a brutally hard day in the saddle are still expected.

item.title: After Remco Evenepoel, Tim Wellens also withdraws from Belgian Championships
item.description: Defending champion Tim Wellens has also withdrawn from the Belgian road championships on Sunday. Wout van Aert will also not start and, due to the after-effects of his elbow injury, will not ride the Tour de France either.

item.description: Extreme heat has significantly affected the Czech and Slovak national championships in Jeseník. item.title: Julia Kopecký wins Czech road title after sprinting past Zemanová

Viktória Chladoňová won the Slovak title, finishing just ahead of the Czech pair in the overall standings. Among junior women, Karolína Špicarová defended her national title.

item.title: Alec Segaert beats UAE stars to win Belgian time trial title
item.description: After a few disappointing time trials, Alec Segaert bounced back to win the Belgian time trial championship, beating UAE riders Tim Wellens and Florian Vermeersch. Top 5: Alec Segaert 24:56, Tim Wellens +0:06, Vlad Van Mechelen +0:14, Rune Herregodts +0:15, Sander De Pestel +0:16.

item.title: 22-year-old Kristian Egholm wins Danish time trial title in major upset
item.description: There were several surprises at the Danish time trial championships. In the women’s race, triathlete Sif Nikoline Bendix Madsen won with a time of 37:08.58. In the men’s race, 22-year-old Kristian Egholm of Lidl-Trek Future Racing caused the biggest upset, winning in 33:20.54. Men’s top 3: Kristian Egholm (Lidl-Trek Future Racing) 33:20.54, Mikkel Norsgaard Bjerg (UAE Team Emirates-XRG) +0:38, Peter Øxenberg Hansen (INEOS Grenadiers) +5:10. Women’s top 3: Sif Nikoline Bendix Madsen (Aalborg Cykle-Ring) 37:08.58, Gertrud Riis Madsen (Team Aalborg Sparekassen Danmark Women) +9:36, Silje Weitze Antvorskov (Team FRIIS) +1:05.39.

Florian Lipowitz and Remco Evenepoel will lead the team, while Giro d'Italia third-place finisher Jai Hindley will support them in the mountains. Sprinter Jordi Meeus was left out of the selection.

item.title: Bike fitting at Posedla in Varnsdorf: useful rather than a gimmick
item.description: In Varnsdorf, in the Šluknov promontory, POSEDLA makes custom saddles. Choosing a saddle is often a tricky process, and bike fitting is part of the full setup. At Posedla, bike fitting is closely linked to its saddle service, with LIBOR BARTŮNĚK in charge.

item.title: Unexpected French time trial champion Célia Le Mouël: "It exceeds all my expectations"
item.description: Célia Le Mouël (Ma Petite Entreprise) pulled off a huge surprise by becoming French time trial champion on Thursday in La Tour-du-Pin (Isère), ahead of the favorites Maëva Squiban (2nd), Cédrine Kerbaol (3rd) and Juliette Berthet (4th). In her press conference, and before putting on her tricolor jersey that she will wear this summer at the Tour de France, the 25-year-old Breton could hardly believe it.

item.title: Tour de France: Oscar Onley ruled out with significant shoulder injury
item.description: Oscar Onley has been ruled out of next month’s Tour de France with a shoulder injury, his team Netcompany-Ineos confirmed.

item.title: Tour de France has started in Spain twice before: San Sebastián in 1992 and Bilbao in 2023
item.description: The Tour de France 2026 will start in Barcelona, making it the third time the Grand Départ is held in Spain after San Sebastián in 1992 and Bilbao in 2023.

item.title: USA Cycling announces team for 2026 UCI Para-Cycling Road World Championships
item.description: The World Championships will be held on home soil in Huntsville, Alabama, from September 4-7, 2026.

item.title: Dutch mountain bike team heads to La Thuile World Cup with confidence
item.description: La Thuile is one of the new World Cup venues this season. The Italian village on the southeast side of the Mont Blanc massif has hosted regular mountain bike races in recent years, and from July 3 to 5 it will stage XCC and XCO races at the highest level. The Dutch mountain bike team is ready for it.

item.title: Vacek wins third straight national time trial title, with Otruba second and Kelemen third
item.description: There were no surprises at the national championships in Uničov. Mathias Vacek was clearly the best in the 48-kilometer time trial and claimed the elite title for the third year in a row. Jakub Otruba took his fifth straight medal, while Petr Kelemen finished third. In the Slovak race, Lukáš Kubiš defended his title.

item.title: Alberto Monti: 'I was worried about the extreme heat. It is easy to overheat. We did everything to prevent that. Everyone in the team is thrilled!'
item.description: Alberto Monti only began racing seriously when he moved into the elite ranks. He joined ATT Investments in mid-2024 at age 20, after barely racing as a junior. That may be one reason for his rapid progress. This year he has already collected strong results in France, Greece, Austria, Poland and at home, including the Peace Race U23. His victory in the national U23 time trial is, in his view, the biggest moment of his short career so far.

item.title: Drama in Uničov as Julia Kopecký defends national time trial title against Kvasničková, with Chladoňová best among Slovaks
item.description: Julia Kopecký won the Czech time trial championship for the third time in her career and defended her national title despite heavy pressure from Eliška Kvasničková. The VIF Cycling Team also had success in Uničov, winning four medals in the Czech and Slovak events. Viktória Chladoňová won the Slovak elite time trial as expected.

item.title: Monti wins U23 time trial title, with Strejček and Šumpík on the podium
item.description: Alberto Monti of ATT Investments is the new national U23 time trial champion. On the 48-kilometer course in Uničov, he had no rival and beat second-placed Adam Strejček by 1:14 to claim his first title.

item.title: Drama in Uničov as Julie Kopecký defends national time trial title against Kvasničková, with Chladoňová best among Slovaks
item.description: Julie Kopecký won the Czech time trial championship for the third time in her career and defended her national title despite heavy pressure from Eliška Kvasničková. The VIF Cycling Team also had success in Uničov, winning four medals in the Czech and Slovak events. Viktória Chladoňová won the Slovak elite time trial as expected.

item.title: Roman Kreuziger Cycling Academy celebrates a double as Daniel Kuběna becomes Czech junior champion and Timotej Michalík wins in Slovakia
item.description: The junior national time trial championships went as expected, but that did not make them any less dramatic. In the Czech race, Kuběna (RKCA) and Zaňka (Dukla) took the top two places, while another RKCA rider, Timotej Michalík, became Slovak champion. The 17-year-old showed strong form at the International Cycling Days at Ncup, but his best time may still have been a surprise. RKCA can be pleased with its work: all six Czech riders finished in the top 15, and all three Slovak riders finished in the top five.

item.title: Onley will miss the Tour de France after crash at Tour Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes
item.description: Oscar Onley was set to lead Netcompany Ineos in the general classification at this year's Tour de France, but he will not be in the lineup. The fourth-placed rider from last year's Tour is still dealing with a shoulder injury, is not training as planned, and will not be able to start.

item.title: Jakub Zaňka and Daniel Kuběna are the favourites for the junior national time trial title
item.description: The joint Czech and Slovak national championships begin today with the individual time trials for men, women, U23 riders, junior women and junior men. The junior race has 82 entrants, including 23 Slovak and 59 Czech riders. The main favourites are Jakub Zaňka and Daniel Kuběna.

item.title: Kasper crypto4me targets medals at the national championships
item.description: Kasper crypto4me, the most successful team of recent years, heads into the national championships with medal ambitions. Last year, they were the only team besides Mathias Vacek to win at least two medals. Sport director Petr Kaltofen said a medal would be the main goal again, with active racing the priority.

item.title: Denmark's biggest cycling stars gather for the Danish Championships during DIF DM-ugen
item.description: When DIF DM-ugen comes to Herning, the city will host the Danish road cycling championships for both women and men. On Friday and Sunday, some of the country's strongest riders will compete over two days of top-level national racing for the Danish titles. Friday features the time trial, with a 29.2-kilometer course starting and finishing at Torvet in Herning. The first rider starts at 3:30 p.m., with the U23 and elite women contesting the national titles before the men finish the day on Friday evening. Sunday brings the road race, traditionally one of the biggest national races of the year, with many of Denmark's leading international riders competing for the right to call themselves Danish champion. The start lists are strong, and spectators can look forward to seeing Danish top-level cycling up close as riders fight for medals, titles, and the right to wear the Dannebrog jersey for the coming year as reigning Danish champion. The Danish Championships are one of the most important recurring events in Danish cycling and a central part of DIF DM-ugen. The races also offer a unique chance to bring Danish cycling together and closer to the public. You can see start lists, times, and other relevant information here: Friday: https://www.sportstiming.dk/event/17431 Sunday: https://www.sportstiming.dk/event/17432

item.title: Shock before the national championships: women's defending champion Kristýna Burlová will not race in Jeseník
item.description: Czech champion Kristýna Burlová will not defend her title this year. One of the biggest stars of the domestic championships had to abandon both the time trial and, eventually, the road race after a crash in Spain. The national championships in Jeseník will therefore be without the reigning winner just as one of the toughest races in recent years is expected.

item.title: Vacek looks ahead to the home championships but expects no help
item.description: Mathias Vacek is widely expected to leave this year's Czech Republic championships in Uničov and Jeseník with both titles. He is the clear favorite in the time trial, and only bad luck is likely to stop him from winning. The road race will be more difficult, but as he showed last year, he can handle a complicated race even without team support.

item.title: Updated: What is the Jeseník national championships circuit like? Possibly easier than it looks
item.description: The organizers of the joint Czech and Slovak championships announced 4,000 meters of climbing, but maps show 3,360 meters. So what is the Jeseník national championships circuit like? Yes, it is hard, but Mathias Vacek will not have a guaranteed victory. The course does not rule out tactical advantages for larger teams, as has often been the case in the past.

item.title: Time for the European BMX Championships
item.description: The BMX season is well underway, and after two strong World Cups and a good European Cup in the spring, attention now turns to the European Championships in France. Danish riders have already shown well in the first four World Cup rounds, reaching the final in four races across categories. The European Championships will be held in Sarrians, which also hosted the first two World Cup rounds in early June. Malene Kejlstrup took her best World Cup result there with second place. National coach Klaus Bøgh Bonde says he is looking forward to the event and has selected the team based mainly on current form. The Danish team for the 2026 European Championships in Sarrians is: Elite women: Malene Kejlstrup; U23 women: Bolette Schlütter; Junior women: Ida Østergaard; Elite men: Magnus Dyhre, Jakob Sardag; U23 men: Jonas Idskov, Rasmus Simonsen; Junior men: William Garner, Jeppe Kejlstrup.

item.title: U23 Danish TT and DEMIN Cup in Skælskør: a major test against the wind
item.description: Skælskør hosted several major races on the same windy, sunlit course: the men's U23 Danish time trial championship and the second round of the DEMIN Cup for men and women. In the U23 race, Mads Landbo retained his title with an average speed of more than 52.11 km/h over 24.7 km. Kristian Egholm and Alfred Christensen completed the podium. In the women's DEMIN Cup time trial, Gertrud Riis Madsen won ahead of Alberte Greve and Julie Marckmann. Silje Weitze Antvorskov remains in the overall lead, while Ida Dam Fialla took the white youth jersey. In the men's DEMIN Cup, Ville Merløv kept the overall lead, with Mads Landbo and Mathias Larsen tied for second. The next round will be held on Bornholm on 22 August.

item.title: Beth Shriever leads Great Britain Cycling Team for 2026 UEC BMX Racing European Championships

item.title: Michael Kukrle wins third Czech gravel cup round in Proseč after long jury debate
item.description: The winner of the third race in the Czech gravel cup was not decided until the sprint to the finish gate at Cyklo Maštale in Proseč on the Bohemian-Moravian Highlands. The result was then debated for almost an hour after the finish. Road rider Michael Kukrle (Kasper crypto4me) was declared the winner, while off-road specialist Filip Adel (Superior) was left disappointed.

item.title: Mountain bikes move to 32-inch wheels as Lenzerheide World Series confirms form
item.description: The debut of a bike with 32-inch wheels was the main talking point of round four of the mountain bike World Series in Lenzerheide. It also marked a further step back for Czech riders from their recent positions.

item.title: Mathias Enghuus appointed new mechanic for the Danish national team
item.description: Danmarks Cykle Union has hired Mathias Enghuus as mechanic for the Danish national team. He is already a familiar face at Danmarks Cykle Union after many years of volunteer work with the road national teams. His experience with DCU already includes the European Championships, World Championships and many trips with the U23 national team. Elite chief Morten Bennekou is pleased that Enghuus will join as full-time mechanic in August. Enghuus will take up the role on 1 August 2026.

item.title: Historic Fyn setting could host the World Championships
item.description: Denmark has submitted a bid to the UCI to host the 2031 Cyclocross World Championships, with Egeskov Slot on Fyn as the proposed venue. The bid is backed by Sport Event Denmark, Faaborg-Midtfyn Kommune and Destination Fyn, and highlights the setting around Egeskov Slot, which also hosts Heartland Festival. If successful, the event would bring about 300 cyclocross riders, 20,000 spectators and 300 accredited media to the area. Organisers say the bid builds on Denmark's cycling culture and its experience hosting major events, including Copenhagen Sprint and the 2019 Cyclocross World Championships in Bogense.

item.title: Leen Massaer: 'It is a real added value to have UCI races in Belgium'
Future Racing supports cycling kids and youth at Copenhagen Sprint
item.description:
Future Racing returns this year as part of Copenhagen Sprint. It is a short, intense youth race on the closed circuit in Copenhagen, which the professionals also race on Saturday and Sunday. The race gives children and young riders in the U11-U17 age groups from local cycling clubs the chance to experience real race atmosphere, competition and community in a safe setting. After last year's success, organisers hope to see even more spectators along the course this year. Extra support from the crowd makes a big difference for the young riders and creates a festive setting that motivates and delights the participants.
Future Racing is not only about crossing the line first, but about giving the next generation of cyclists a unique experience. Riders get to race on a closed circuit in central Copenhagen with short, explosive laps where pace, technique and nerves all matter. For spectators, it is a great chance to get close to the sport, watch emerging talent in action and help inspire more children and young people to take up cycling.
The race takes place on Sunday before the professional field reaches the circuit. The first riders start at 10:30, so arrive early, choose a spot with a good view of the finish or a corner, and bring family and friends. Applause and encouragement mean a lot to the young riders. Check practical details such as start times and route information on Sportstiming: www.sportstiming.dk/event/18537.

item.title: Men's U23 road race championship heads to Vejen

The race has often highlighted future Danish stars, with former winners and podium finishers including Rasmus Søjberg, Julius Johansen, Cristopher Juul-Jensen, Magnus Cort and Casper Pedersen. Reigning champion Alfred Mørch Kongstad of Airtox - Carl Ras returns to defend his title. The hilly course is expected to be made harder by wind and rain. Start is at 11:40, the finish is expected at 15:40, with the podium ceremony at 16:10.

item.title: National teams set for Copenhagen Sprint
item.description: The national teams will line up at Copenhagen Sprint on June 13-14, as the race returns for its second edition. Women’s national coach Mette Andersen has selected Amalie Dideriksen, Christina Bragh Lorenzen, Ellen Klinge, Laura Auerbach-Lind, Olympia Norrid-Mortensen and Silje Weitze Antvorskov. Andersen says the goal is to support Amalie Dideriksen in the finale. For the men, national coach Michael Mørkøv has selected Alexander Salby, Conrad Haugsted, Frederik Rodenberg, Mads Andersen, Rasmus Bøgh Wallin, Sebastian Nielsen and Tobias Aagaard. Mørkøv says the team will try to make its mark on the race and test itself against the world elite on home roads.

Peter Schulting won the sprint for second, but could not deliver a home victory for the Hanzerenners. Škoda BørneTour becomes part of PostNord Danmark Rundt
item.description: When Denmark's biggest stage race rolls through the country this summer, it will also bring extra focus on community, experiences, and cycling joy for the youngest riders. In 2026, Škoda BørneTour will be integrated into PostNord Danmark Rundt with events in selected start towns. Children will be able to get on their bikes and experience the atmosphere of a bike race on the same day and in the same setting as some of the world's best riders. The events will take place in four start towns: Aalborg on 29 July, Glyngøre on 30 July, Fredericia on 31 July, and Køge on 2 August. Each event starts shortly after the professional riders have set off on the day's stage, giving children the chance to ride the route in a safe, closed-off setting. The PostNord Danmark Rundt events are for children aged 3-10. This differs from the regular BørneTour format, which usually targets children and young people aged 3-15. The aim of Škoda BørneTour is to inspire future elite cyclists and create positive experiences with cycling at the center. The events are run with local youth cycling clubs, which will also offer introductory opportunities for interested children and young people. More information is available on the event registration pages and on Škoda BørneTour's website and social media channels.

Strong Danish results in international cycling weekend
item.description: Danish riders delivered strong results across track, road and mountain bike racing over the weekend. At GP Framar on the track, Robin Skivild finished third in the men's elite omnium, while Oskar Winkler was 17th. Laura Auerbach-Lind took third in the women's omnium and Anna Hansen finished seventh. In the points race, Robin Skivild was 10th and Oskar Winkler 20th in the men's elite event, while Laura Auerbach-Lind finished eighth and Anna Hansen 24th in the women's race. In the scratch race, Robin Skivild was 12th and Oskar Winkler 15th, while Laura Auerbach-Lind won the women's race and Laura Hansen finished 11th. Oskar Winkler and Robin Skivild also took second in the men's madison. At the 54th Course de la Paix Juniors, Malthe Risbjerg finished second overall after winning stage two, and Julius Birkedal was third overall. Jakob Resen and Rasmus Helt Hansen also performed well. At the Nordic mountain bike championships in Sweden, Sebastian Fini Juhl won the men's elite title and Caroline Bohé won the women's elite title. In the junior men's race, Johannes Brædstrup-Holm was second, Gustav Freitag Laursen third, Anton Hermann Nielsen fourth and Villads Nyegaard fifth. In the junior women's race, Caroline Matilde Hede was second and Rose-Mai Damsgaard fifth. In the men's U23 race, Hector Glud Hjorth finished eighth. In the women's U23 race, Julie Lillelund won, Freja Dissing Andersen was second, Sara Aaboe Kallestrup fourth and Smilla Zoffmann Glud sixth.

item.title: National selections: Nations' Cup Men Juniors - Course de la Paix
Danes ready for Giro d’Italia 2026
item.description: The Giro d’Italia starts on Friday 8 May, with the peloton set for three weeks of hard racing for the iconic pink leader’s jersey. This year’s race begins in Bulgaria, where the first three stages will be held before the race moves on to Italy. Over the next three weeks, riders will cover 3,468 kilometres and tackle 48,700 metres of climbing. Eight Danish riders will line up in this year’s Giro d’Italia, and for several of them it will be a special debut. Four of the Danish riders will make their Giro d’Italia debut, while both Johan Price-Pejtersen and Niklas Larsen will race their first Grand Tour. Danish starters: Jonas Vingegaard (Team Visma | Lease a Bike), Tobias Lund Andresen (Decathlon AG2R La Mondiale), Rasmus Søjberg (Decathlon AG2R La Mondiale), Johan Price-Pejtersen (Alpecin-Deceuninck), Michael Valgren (EF Education-EasyPost), Christopher Juul-Jensen (Team Jayco AlUla), Niklas Larsen (Unibet Tietema Rockets), Mikkel Bjerg (UAE Team Emirates). For Jonas Vingegaard, it will also be his first time starting the Giro d’Italia, and it will be interesting to follow the Dane through the three demanding weeks. Riders such as Mikkel Bjerg, Michael Valgren and Christopher Juul-Jensen bring valuable experience to the race, while several younger Danish riders get the chance to experience cycling’s biggest stage for the first time. There will also be Danish Grand Tour debuts for Johan Price-Pejtersen and Niklas Larsen, who are both set for their first WorldTour stage race on the biggest stage. For Niklas Larsen, it is a special step in his career. Larsen comes from the Danish track national team and has for several years been an important part of Denmark’s success in the 4,000-metre team pursuit, where the country has won medals and international top results. Now a very different challenge awaits on the road, as he takes on three hard weeks in the Giro d’Italia. Johan Price-Pejtersen will also make his Grand Tour debut after strong performances on both the time trial bike and the road. For both riders, the Giro will be a chance to gain valuable experience at the very highest level. Three dramatic weeks await. The Giro d’Italia is known as one of cycling’s hardest and most unpredictable races. Long mountain finishes, technical descents, tactical stages and explosive finales create plenty of drama over the three weeks. With eight Danes on the start line, another exciting Grand Tour is in store from a Danish perspective.

item.title: Save money, leave the car behind and start cycling again
item.description: Fuel prices never seem to stop rising, but your bike can help ease the pressure. It is a cheap way to get from A to B, especially if it has been sitting unused for a while.

item.title: German Cycling
Dutch National Cyclocross Championships 2027 to be held in Gemert
item.description: The Dutch National Championships for novices, juniors and U23/elite will be held in Gemert in 2027. It will be a special edition, as the national title race will take place entirely in the centre of the Brabant village. It will be a true city-centre cross, with the iconic Kasteel Gemert as the backdrop. The event is organised by R&TC Buitenlust.

item.title: Danish results at WHOOP UCI MTB World Series
item.description: The first round of the WHOOP UCI MTB World Series took place in South Korea at the weekend, where the Danish riders had a challenging start to the season. Conditions were demanding, with a technical course and difficult circumstances that tested technique, endurance and the ability to adapt on the fly. Even so, the Danish riders delivered several solid performances across the disciplines. Elite – Cross Country: In short track (XCC), Sebastian Fini Juul finished 16th, while Simon Andreassen and Tobias Lillelund placed 34th and 35th respectively. In the Olympic discipline (XCO), Sebastian Fini Juhl delivered a strong result with 9th place. Simon Andreassen finished 27th, while Tobias Lillelund came 53rd. In the women’s race, Sofie Heby Pedersen finished 15th in short track and 32nd in XCO. U23 – strong Danish podium result: In the U23 class, there was particular Danish success in short track, where Gustav Heby Pedersen rode to an excellent 2nd place and secured a podium finish. Mikkel Lose finished 11th, while Nikolaj Hougs was 15th. In the XCO discipline, Gustav Heby Pedersen finished 18th, Nikolaj Hougs 20th, while Mikkel Lose unfortunately did not finish. Downhill – junior women: In junior women’s downhill, Rosa Mari Jensen delivered a strong performance. She qualified 2nd and followed up with a fine 5th place in the final.

item.title: New opportunities for the mountain bike selection at Junior Series Heubach
item.description: A true classic on the calendar, but also a fair race with long climbs and wide roads that offer plenty of opportunities to overtake. That is the best way to describe the HC race in Heubach, also the next stop in the Junior Series. It is a good chance for the KNWU selection to show what they can do, especially with the first European mountain bike World Cup in Nove Mesto a week later.

Before the summer holidays, riders can take part in pump track events at six different locations in the National Pump Track Series (NPS), and the fight for the national title will take place in Oss on 6 September at the Dutch National Championships.

item.description: Nineteen-year-old prospect Paul Seixas will make his Tour de France debut this summer, with ambitions of riding for the general classification.

item.title: Second round of the Shimano MTB Ligaen completed
item.description: The second round of the Shimano MTB Ligaen took place in the technical trails of Rold Skov, where riders were tested on technique, pace, and endurance. The course featured plenty of turns, roots, and speed, and despite the demanding conditions, there were strong performances across all categories. In the open classes, Jonathan Oxholm Rasmussen (Hammel Cykle Klub) won the 4.2 km race ahead of William Torbensen (Aarhus MTB) and Tobias Kjelsmark Nielsen (Mountain Bike Klub Silkeborg). In the 5.8 km race, Hector Guld Hjorth (Holte MTB Klub) was strongest, with Kristian Østergaard Andersen (miKKen Cycling) and Paw Kragh (Racing Bjerringbro) behind him. In the youth categories, Thorleif Tejlmand Rasmussen (Holte MTB Klub) won U11 boys and Lara Sofia Düring Hansen (Mountainbike Club Vejle) won U11 girls. Jonathan Oxholm Rasmussen again won U13 boys ahead of William Torbensen and Tobias Kjelsmark Nielsen, while Maia Zeeberg Rod Kristensen (HMIF) won U13 girls ahead of Maya Holm-Denninger and Ellen Krogh Skammelsen. Patrick Houlborg Jørgensen (MTB Randers) won U15 boys and Laura Filippa Posselt Gamborg (Hammel Cykle Klub) won U15 girls. Malte Kempel Christensen (MTB Randers) won U17 boys, while Esther Mejdal Haaning (Mountainbike Club Vejle) won U17 girls. In the junior U19 races, Johannes Brædstrup-Holm (Holte MTB Klub) won the men’s race and Caroline Matilde Hede (Holte MTB Klub) won the women’s race. In the elite races, Hector Guld Hjorth (Holte MTB Klub) won the men’s category ahead of Kristian Østergaard Andersen and Paw Kragh, while Julie Lillelund (Mountainbike Club Vejle) won the women’s elite race ahead of Sara Aaboe Kallestrup (MTB Randers) and Freja Dissing Andersen (Holte MTB Klub). Storm Siig Buus (Rold Skov MTB) won the B class, Jens Brokær (Hareskov Cykelklub) won H40, Anne Mi Svarrer (Holte MTB Klub) won D40, Michael Gamborg (Hammel Cykle Klub) won H50, Bente Kortbæk Kibsgaard (Viborg MTB) won D50, and Kåre Winther Hansen (Dansk Mountainbike Klub) won H60.

item.title: Dramatic GP Herning and strong Danish performances
item.description: The weekend featured two of Denmark’s biggest one-day races, GP Herning and Fyen Rundt, with the Danish national team delivering strong performances in both. GP Herning, often called the Danish Paris-Roubaix, was once again a hard and unpredictable race. Kristian Egholm and Gustav Wang were active at the front, and Egholm spent time in a strong solo breakaway that built a lead of around 40 seconds. The race then took a dramatic turn when the peloton was sent the wrong way, leading to a neutralisation and restart that hurt Egholm’s advantage. After the restart, he fought back but was caught with about 40 kilometres to go by a smaller group that also included Wang. In the finale, Wang stayed near the front and contested the sprint, finishing second behind Mads Landbo (ColoQuick). Top 3 GP Herning: Mads Landbo (ColoQuick), Gustav Wang (Danmarks landshold), Frederik Gustav Dahl (Give Steel). The following day, strong Danish form continued at Fyen Rundt, where the national team was again active at the front. Wang was once more among the most aggressive riders and joined a breakaway of about 10 riders that was later reduced to a smaller lead group. He attacked in the final kilometres but was caught just before the finish. Top 3 Fyen Rundt: Mads Andersen (Swatt Club), Sebastian Nielsen (Team ColoQuick), Pelle Køster (Airtox-Carl Ras).

The World Cup series features 14 races across the season, with the world’s best mountain bikers competing in different disciplines from Friday to Sunday. Although the riders compete for their respective teams rather than for Denmark, there is still strong Danish representation in several categories. item.title: Selected: Squad for GP Herning and Fyen Rundt

The weekend features two races with different demands, from the open roads around Herning to the more hilly and tactically demanding routes on Funen. With good weather expected, the stage is set for a fast and aggressive race from the start. Both races are known for creating selection along the way and place high demands on positioning, teamwork and race reading. GP Herning – 02.05.2026: Henrik Breiner Pedersen, Alexander Salby, Gustav Wang, Kristian Egholm, Kevin Biehl, Johan Eltoft. Fyen Rundt – 03.05.2026: Henrik Breiner Pedersen, Alexander Salby, Gustav Wang, Kristian Egholm, Kevin Biehl, Noah Andersen. item.title: Škoda BørneTour gets underway
item.description: The Škoda BørneTour season got underway in Tårnby on Saturday, with strong winds shaping the day. Despite the blustery conditions, many children turned up in high spirits and with plenty of enthusiasm for cycling. There was a great atmosphere along the route, with riders and spectators helping create a festive start to the tour. Children of all ages gave it their all on their bikes and showed that the most important thing is not necessarily finishing first, but taking part and having fun. Škoda BørneTour is a cycling race for children aged 3 to 15 and is aimed at both beginners and more experienced young riders. The races are held on short, safe circuits in towns across the country from May to September. The focus is on community, exercise and the joy of cycling, and everyone can join regardless of speed or ambition. Škoda BørneTour travels around the country, and if you could not take part in Tårnby, there will be another chance next time. The next stop is Middelfart, where the race will also be part of the cycling festival around Fyen Rundt. All events can be found here, where registration is also possible: https://www.sportstiming.dk/bornetour/events

item.title: Gravel, wind and strong performances
item.description:
The weekend’s Cykelgear Gravel Cup round brought challenging conditions in Store Dyrehave, where the wind shaped the race. That did not stop the riders, who turned out in large numbers and delivered strong performances on the demanding gravel routes.
Despite the wind, the racing was aggressive from the start, and several riders managed to set a high pace through the forest’s technical sections and open stretches.
In the 10.3 km class, Karoline Kirkegaard (Holstebro Cycleclub af 1960) took the win ahead of Mie Houlind Lynge and Jeppe Lynge, all from the same club.
In the 22.7 km class, Emil Toudal (Team Cranks) won, with Simon Bak (Team Give Steel) and Ian Millennium (Cykling Odense) following.
There were also strong performances in the youth categories. Jeppe Lynge won U11 boys, while Karoline Kirkegaard and Mie Houlind Lynge took first and second in U13 girls.
In U17 boys, Felix Wurtz Ingvardsen (Randers Cykleklub af 1910) won ahead of Jens Christian Schmidt and Gustav Nordby Hallasmøller.
In U19 men, Andreas Bay Lastein (Mountainbike Club Vejle) took the win, followed by Felix Bering and Jacob Dahlstrøm Vinter.
In the age categories, the racing was equally strong. In men AG19-34, Thorkild Bruun (Aarhus Studenternes Cykelklub) won, while Alberte Olesen Larsen took the women’s race.
In men AG35-39, Daniel Wollbrecht (Holbæk Cykelsport) won, and in AG40-44 it was Peter Thidemann (Aalborg Mountainbikeklub) who took first place.
In men AG45-49, Troels Halfeld Nielsen won, while Anne-Mi Svarrer won the women’s category.
In men AG50-54, Danny Jonasson took the win, while Else Lund Andersen won among the women.
In men AG55-59, Lars Grøndahl Schou was strongest, while Morten Egholm won AG60-64.
In AG65-69, Mats Carlsson won, and in AG70+, Benny Andersson took the victory. All results can be seen here: https://www.sportstiming.dk/event/17908/results?round=d10300

item.title: Strong Danish performances at BMX races in Czech Republic
item.description:
Round 5 and 6 of the UEC BMX series were held in Czech Republic over the weekend, and the Danish riders delivered several strong performances across the categories.
The races were run in good but demanding conditions, where technique, explosiveness and timing were all crucial on the track. The Danish riders impressed against international competition and made their mark in several finals.
Round 5 – solid Danish results
On Saturday, April 25, round 5 was held, with several Danes in action.
In the junior class, Ida Østergaard started and rode a steady race to secure a strong 5th place in a competitive field.
In U23, Jonas Idskov delivered a strong performance. With good speed and clean heats, he rode to an excellent 4th place.
In the elite class, both Malene Kejlstrup and Jakob Sardag were on the start list. Malene showed a high level throughout the race and finished 5th, while Jakob Sardag also put in a solid effort and crossed the line 8th in the final.
Round 6 – podium for Malene
On Sunday, April 26, round 6 was held, and the Danish riders were again ready to race.
Malene Kejlstrup produced the best Danish result of the weekend. After a well-executed race through heats and finals, she secured 2nd place and a podium finish.
In the same race, Magnus Dyhre also delivered a strong performance and finished 4th, just outside the podium.
A strong weekend in international company
Overall, it was a weekend with several top results and good Danish performances in Czech Republic. The Danish riders showed they can compete at a high international level and gained valuable experience in a strong field.

item.title: National selections: UEC BMX European Cup 5&6 Benátky nad Jizerou
Races on the schedule: Gravel in Hillerød and a Monument in Belgium
item.description:
The weekend features racing on several fronts, both on Danish gravel roads and in the Belgian hills.
On Saturday, April 25, round 2 of the Cykelgear Gravel Cup will be held in Store Dyrehave near Hillerød, where riders from across the country will gather for another intense day on gravel.
In cooperation with Danmarks Cykle Union and DCU’s gravel sponsors — Cykelgear, CeramicSpeed, Schwalbe, Sweet Protection and Shimano — Hillerød Cycle Club invites riders to a day of speed, technique and challenging routes in scenic surroundings.
See more about the event here: https://www.sportstiming.dk/event/17908
Danes ready for Liège–Bastogne–Liège
On Sunday, one of cycling’s most iconic classics takes place when the peloton lines up for Liège–Bastogne–Liège, also known as La Doyenne, the oldest of all Monuments.
Five Danish riders are ready to take on the demanding race, where endurance and climbing strength will be decisive.
The following Danes will start: Mikkel Honoré (EF Education–EasyPost) Magnus Cort (Uno-X Mobility) Andreas Kron (Uno-X Mobility) Mattias Skjelmose (Lidl-Trek) Anders Foldager (Jayco AlUla)
A classic with history
Liège–Bastogne–Liège was first held in 1892 and is the oldest of cycling’s five Monuments.
The race is more than 250 km long. It includes a long series of short, steep climbs. It is often decided on the final climbs.
Among the best-known ascents are La Redoute and Roche-aux-Faucons, which often prove decisive.
A race for the strongest
The race is known as one of the toughest on the calendar and favours riders with both climbing ability and endurance. Unlike many other classics, it is rarely decided by a sprint; instead, the differences are made on the many climbs in the finale.
With riders such as Skjelmose, Kron and Honoré, Denmark has several cards to play, while Cort and Foldager could also play an important role in how the race unfolds.
A long and hard day in the Belgian hills awaits, where the Danish riders will have the chance to make their mark against the world elite.

item.title: Danish BMX riders set for UEC rounds 5 and 6 in Benátky, Czech Republic
item.description: Several Danish riders are in action this weekend as both the talent squad and national team riders line up in different BMX categories. Assistant talent coach Kenneth Rose is attending with the talent squad, which includes Ida Østergaard in junior women and Viktor Bille, William Garner, William Lund and William Boel in junior men. Five of the six national team riders are also racing. Jonas Idskov and Rasmus Riise are entered in U23, Jakob Sardag and Magnus Dyhre are on the elite men’s start list, and Malene Kejlstrup will race elite women. It should be a strong weekend for Danish BMX across several classes.

item.title: National selections: Track World Cup in Nilai
Junior selection for Nations Cup race Course de la Paix
item.description: The Course de la Paix takes place in the Czech Republic from 7 to 10 May. Six junior men will race for the Dutch selection in this multi-day Nations Cup event.

item.title: Danish junior women ready for Omloop van Borsele
item.description: The Danish junior women are set to begin one of the most traditional stage races when they line up for Omloop van Borsele in the Netherlands. The race, also known as “Junior Vrouwen”, is run over three stages and brings together some of Europe’s best junior riders for an intense and educational competition. The selected riders are Maia Howell, Sarah Møller Madsen, Lærke Mikkelsen, Maja Klöcker-Larsen and Freja Bjørn Olesen. Over the three stages, the riders will be tested in different disciplines, with pace, positioning and race awareness all coming into play. The race typically combines a time trial and road stages, demanding versatility. For the Danish riders, it is a chance to measure themselves against strong international competition and gain valuable stage-race experience on the way to senior level.

item.title: Less than two weeks until Fyen Rundt: strong elite field and open to amateurs
item.description: There are now less than two weeks to go until Fyen Rundt 2026, which will be held on Sunday 3 May, and the start list already points to a strong and exciting elite field in Middelfart. The Danish national team, led by Michael Mørkøv, will line up with several big names, including Carl-Frederik Bévort, a former winner of the race, Gustav Wang, a former junior world time trial champion, and Alexander Salby, who is in impressive form. In recent weeks, Salby has delivered strong results in Asia with a stage win in the Tour of Hainan as well as stage wins and the overall victory in the Tour of Thailand. The Danish continental teams also bring strong line-ups. Team ColoQuick will feature Tobias Aagaard Hansen, who has medals from both the European and World Track Championships and a previous podium finish in Fyen Rundt. Reffen CO:PLAY – Giant Store is also on the start list, with former winner Lasse Norman Leth among its leading riders. Several riders also have a special connection to the organising club, Cykling Odense. Tobias Aagaard Hansen, Alexander Salby and Lasse Norman Leth have all previously raced for the club, adding an extra local dimension when they return to the roads of Funen. Fyen Rundt is Denmark’s oldest and the world’s third-oldest still existing cycling race. Dating back to 1894, it is a long-standing tradition that each year brings together elite riders, amateurs and spectators around the sport. Amateur riders can still take part, with routes of 74, 113 and 177 kilometres on offer. Along the way there are feed zones with refreshments and energy products, and a well-earned burger awaits at the finish. There is also a children’s race on the programme, making it a day for the whole family. With start and finish in Middelfart, the race is easy to reach for participants and spectators from across the country.

item.title: German Cycling
Dutch riders for World Cup para-cycling races in Gistel and Abruzzo
item.description: The Dutch para-cycling road season begins next week with a European double header in the World Cup. Racing takes place in Gistel, Belgium, from 28 April to 1 May, before the riders travel a week later to Pescara on the Italian coast in the Abruzzo region. Around 50 countries are represented, with 475 participants across the Tricycling, Tandem, Cycling and handbike categories. The Netherlands will have 30 riders at the start in Belgium and 20 in Italy.

item.title: La Flèche Wallonne 2026: live coverage
item.description: Follow the live coverage and GPS tracking of La Flèche Wallonne 2026.

item.title: Danes ready for La Flèche Wallonne
item.description: Another spring classic takes place on Wednesday as the peloton lines up for La Flèche Wallonne, a race known for its iconic finish on the Mur de Huy. Several Danish riders are ready to take on the world’s best in this demanding Ardennes race. In the men’s race, Mikkel Bjerg (UAE Team Emirates), Mattias Skjelmose (Lidl-Trek), Albert Withen Philipsen (Lidl-Trek), Anders Foldager (Jayco AlUla), Asbjørn Hellemose (Jayco AlUla), Andreas Kron (Uno-X Mobility), Anthon Charmig (Uno-X Mobility), Alexander Kamp (Uno-X Mobility) and Mikkel Honoré (EF Education–EasyPost) are on the start list. In the women’s race, Cecilie Uttrup Ludwig (CANYON//SRAM zondacrypto) will start. La Flèche Wallonne is especially known for its finish on the Mur de Huy, one of cycling’s most iconic climbs. The Mur de Huy is about 1.3 km long, with an average gradient of around 9-10 percent and the steepest sections exceeding 20 percent. That means the race is almost always decided in the final metres, where explosiveness is crucial. As part of Ardennes week, La Flèche Wallonne sits between Amstel Gold Race and Liège–Bastogne–Liège and typically suits the most explosive climbers. With riders such as Skjelmose, Kron and Honoré, several Danes could play a role in the finale, while the support riders may also prove important. On the women’s side, Cecilie Uttrup Ludwig is an experienced Ardennes rider and could also feature in the finish.

item.title: New USA Cycling programs aim to improve member experience and athlete support
item.description: New and growing initiatives are designed to expand athlete support and add more value to USA Cycling members.

item.title: Shimano MTB Ligaen opens in Roskilde
item.description:
The 2026 Shimano MTB Ligaen got underway in Roskilde with the season’s first round. Denmark’s top mountain bikers battled for the first important points of the year.
In the UCI categories, Mikkel Løse (Lexware Mountainbike Team) won the men’s elite race ahead of Kristian Østergaard Andersen (miKKen Cycling) and Nikolaj Hougs (CUBE Factory Racing).
Julie Lillelund (Mountainbike Club Vejle) won the women’s elite race, with Sara Aaboe Kallestrup (MTB Randers) second and Smilla Zoffmann Glud (Velo Cycling Club) third.
In the men’s U19 race, Anton Hermann Nielsen (Rold Skov MTB) took the win ahead of Johannes Brædstrup-Holm (Holte MTB Klub) and Albert Lundgren (Aarhus MTB). Rose-Mai Damsgaard (Mountainbike Club Vejle) won the women’s U19 race ahead of Caroline Matilde Hede (Holte MTB Klub) and Ida Mahlert (Dansk Mountainbike Klub).
In the open classes, William Torbensen (Aarhus MTB) won the 4.2 km race ahead of Jonathan Oxholm Rasmussen (Hammel Cykle Klub) and Tobias Kjelsmark Nielsen (Mountain Bike Klub Silkeborg). Julie Lillelund also won the 5.8 km race, ahead of Sara Aaboe Kallestrup and Smilla Zoffmann Glud.
Among the younger riders, Thorleif Tejlmand Rasmussen (Holte MTB Klub) won the U11 boys race and Eva Haugaard Friis-Madsen (Dansk Mountainbike Klub) won the U11 girls race. William Torbensen won the U13 boys race, while Maia Zeeberg Rød Kristensen (HMIF) won the girls’ race.
Patrick Houlberg Jørgensen (MTB Randers) won the U15 boys race and Lykke Philipsen (Viborg MTB) won the girls’ race. Storm Banke (Cykling Odense) won the U17 boys race, while Esther Mejdal Haaning (Mountainbike Club Vejle) won the girls’ race.
In the masters categories, Anders Lausten (Holte MTB Klub) won H40 and Anne-Mi Svarrer (Holte MTB Klub) won D40. Allan Månsson (Holte MTB Klub) won H50 and Mette Sofie Bønneland Maysfelt (ED Elite) won D50.
Palle Egeberg Jensen (Hareskov Cykelklub) won H60 ahead of Knud Heglund (Holbæk Cykelsport) and Lasse Petersen (RMK). Anton Skov Reunert (Roskilde Mountainbike Klub) won the B class, while Freja Nilausen (Roskilde Mountainbike Klub) won the DB class ahead of Ditte Clausen (Dansk Mountainbike Klub).
The season is now well underway.

item.title: Two Danish silvers at the World Cup in Hong Kong
item.description:
The Danish track team had a strong weekend at the World Cup in Hong Kong, winning two silver medals and delivering several promising performances against the world’s best.
In the 4 km team pursuit, Robin Skivild, Oskar Winkler, Thor Vangsø and Frederik Rodenberg rode a strong, well-coordinated race to finish second for Denmark.
Amalie Dideriksen also impressed in the omnium, where she won silver. She made her mark with a win in the scratch race and was among the leading riders throughout the event.
Dideriksen also raced the madison with Ellen Klinge, where the pair finished fourth, just outside the medals.
In the men’s omnium, Robin Skivild finished 21st after a strong qualification. In the men’s madison, Oskar Winkler and Robin Skivild placed eighth, while Ellen Klinge finished 18th in the women’s elimination race.

item.title: Shimano MTB Ligaen kicks off in Roskilde
item.description:
The 2026 Shimano MTB Ligaen begins this weekend with the opening round in Roskilde. The series, which features six races across the year, brings together some of Denmark’s best mountain bikers and is the country’s leading MTB series. Riders will compete for points and standings throughout the season.
The opening round includes both XCC (short track) and XCO (cross-country). XCC takes place on Saturday 18 April, while Sunday 19 April features XCO, which tests endurance, technique and strength over a longer distance.
The Roskilde XCO race is also a UCI event, meaning valuable international points are on offer. It will attract a strong field and give Danish riders a chance to measure themselves against high-level competition.
Roskilde Mountainbike Klub is the organiser, and a weekend of speed, technique and racing is expected.

item.title: Danish riders ready for Amstel Gold Race
item.description:
Amstel Gold Race takes place this weekend, with a number of Danish riders set to start in both the men’s and women’s races.
In the men’s race, Denmark is represented by Mattias Skjelmose (Lidl-Trek), Albert Withen Philipsen (Lidl-Trek), Søren Kragh Andersen (Lidl-Trek), Andreas Kron (Uno-X Mobility), Anthon Charmig (Uno-X Mobility), Alexander Kamp (Uno-X Mobility), Mikkel Honoré (EF Education–EasyPost), Mikkel Bjerg (UAE Team Emirates XRG), Asbjørn Hellemose (Jayco AlUla) and Anders Foldager (Jayco AlUla). In the women’s race, Cecilie Uttrup Ludwig (CANYON//SRAM zondacrypto) will start.
Amstel Gold Race is one of the spring classics and marks the start of Ardennes week. The race is known for its many short, steep climbs and technical finales, where positioning and timing are crucial.
The race was first held in 1966.
Last year, Mattias Skjelmose won in a sprint against Remco Evenepoel and Tadej Pogačar, one of the biggest Danish victories in recent years. This year, Mikkel Bjerg returns to Amstel Gold Race for the first time in five years and is aiming for a strong comeback.
With several strong cards, Denmark again has good chances. Skjelmose returns as defending champion, while riders such as Kron, Honoré and Kamp could also play a role in a race that often becomes tactical and unpredictable.
In the women’s race, Cecilie Uttrup Ludwig and Rebecca Koerner will be worth following, as both have the potential to perform well in the demanding Ardennes races.

Danmarks Cykle Union seeks track team mechanic
item.description: Danmarks Cykle Union is looking for a mechanic to support the track national team. The role includes managing the workshop at Ballerup Super Arena, handling all track equipment, traveling with the team at home and abroad, and working closely with staff and equipment partners. Relevant technical experience and knowledge of cycling are considered an advantage.

item.title: Danmarks Cykle Union seeks track national team mechanic
item.description: Danmarks Cykle Union is looking for a mechanic to run the workshop at Ballerup Super Arena and take responsibility for all track equipment for the national track team. The role includes travel with the team at home and abroad, packing before and after training and competition, maintaining workshop and storage facilities, and working closely with DCU staff, equipment partners, and the tech group. Relevant technical training or experience in cycling is an advantage, but not a strict requirement.

item.title: MTB season opens with strong Danish performances on the trails and cobbles
item.description: The weekend brought strong Danish results in both Paris–Roubaix and the opening MTB races in Aabenraa. In Paris–Roubaix, Mads Pedersen (Lidl-Trek) finished in the top 10, while Emma Bjerg Norsgaard (Movistar Team) placed around 35th. In the junior race, Julius Birkedal was 4th and Malthe Risbjerg 8th. The 2026 MTB season began with the DCU XCO & XCC season opener, a UCI C3 XCO race, where Sebastian Fini Juul (Mondraker Factory Team) won the men’s elite race ahead of Hector Glud Hjorth (Holte MTB Klub) and Hector Damgaard (Velo Cycling Club). Elin Båge (IF Hallby Skid) won the women’s elite race as the only starter. Full results followed across youth, junior, masters, and B-class categories.

item.title: Niels Bo Daugaard steps down as director of Danmarks Cykle Union
item.description: Niels Bo Daugaard has left his role as director of Danmarks Cykle Union with immediate effect by mutual agreement. He said he is proud of the commercial partnerships, Ride For Denmark, and the stronger, more professional organisation built during his time at DCU. DCU chairman Morten Anderson thanked him for his dedication and contribution to the professionalisation and strategic development of Danish cycling. The board is now looking for a new director, while the current leadership team continues the work.

item.title: Strong Danish results at the UEC European Cup in Tiel
item.description: The UEC European Cup rounds 3 and 4 were held in Tiel, where Danish BMX riders delivered strong performances and featured near the top in several categories. Malene Kejlstrup was in top form and won Elite Women in both round 3 and round 4, underlining her strong early-season level. In the men’s elite, Magnus Dyhre finished a fine third in round 4, while Jeppe Kejlstrup impressed in Junior Men with second place in round 4. Denmark also had finalists in the elite class. Marcus Leth made his elite debut on Saturday and rode all the way to the final, while Jakob Sardag was also among the participants, reflecting a strong Danish presence in the field. Overall, it was a weekend of excellent Danish results and promising signs for the season ahead.

item.title: Current board re-elected
item.description: At Danmarks Cykle Union's congress on 22 March in Fredericia, the current board was re-elected. Vice president Morten Madsen and board members Jesper Thiesen and Mai Myrhøj Tolstrup were all up for election and were re-elected by acclamation without opponents. The re-election ensures continuity in the work already underway, with a focus on creating calm, direction, and a strong foundation for Danish cycling. With the same team in place, there are good conditions to build on the initiatives already launched and strengthen cooperation across the organisation. Christian Ranneries chose not to stand again for the deputy position so he can focus on track cycling and his work at DBC. The board thanks Christian for his great effort and commitment over time. Sanne Schmidt Bendixen from Randers was elected as the new second deputy. She brings many years of experience in cycling as a rider, commissaire, and volunteer. The board also thanks the representatives, districts, and clubs for the trust shown. The board looks forward to continuing its work and the development of Danish cycling in the coming period.
